g.text: Origins of the synaptic toolkit across evolution
After Colgren & Burkhardt, Nature Reviews Neuroscience 2025
Pre-animal ancestor
SNAREs · GPCRs · iGluRs · Shank · Homer · DLG already present
Choanoﬂagellates
~700 Ma · unicellular · e.g. Monosiga brevicollis
● SNAREs (synaptobrevin, SNAP25, syntaxin), Unc13, complexin, synaptotagmin
● Shank, DLG present (non-synaptic roles); Homer localises to nucleus
● Apical vesicle pool near feeding collar; co-deployment of presynaptic genes
○ No neurexin / neuroligin · ○ No postsynaptic co-deployment or clustering
○ No synaptic ultrastructure
Ctenophora (comb jellies)
~650 Ma · neurons present · possible independent origin · e.g. Mnemiopsis leidyi
● SNAREs, Unc13, complexin, synaptotagmin 7 (not synaptotagmin 1)
● Synapses present: nerve net → eﬀector cells only
● Nerve net is a syncytium — neurons share continuous cytoplasm
○ No true neuroligin; neurexins expressed only in non-neuronal cells
○ No postsynaptic scaﬀolding proteins · ○ No morphologically deﬁned PSD
○ No neuron–neuron chemical synapses
Porifera (sponges)
~620 Ma · no neurons · e.g. Ephydatia muelleri
● Presynaptic genes (Unc13, Rab3, SNAREs) in neuroid cells; protrusions contact choanocytes
● Postsynaptic genes (Shank, Homer, DLG) enriched in choanocytes — bipartite distribution
● Volumetric coordination via glutamate and ATP (sponge "sneezing")
○ No synaptic ultrastructure · ○ No neurexin / neuroligin
Placozoa
~580 Ma · no neurons · e.g. Trichoplax adhaerens
● Full presynaptic secretory machinery: SNAREs, Unc13, RIM, synaptotagmin
● Neuropeptides regulate behaviour; GPCRs co-expressed in peptidergic cells
○ Shank, DLG absent from peptidergic cells · ○ No neuroligin
○ No postsynaptic density ultrastructure · Volumetric paracrine signalling
Cnidaria + Bilateria
~500 Ma · neurons + synapses · e.g. Nematostella, Hydra
● Neurexin (neuronal) + neuroligin assembled — cleft geometry deﬁned (~20 nm)
● PSD-95 / DLG → GKAP → Shank → Homer: full postsynaptic density assembled
● AMPA-R + NMDA-R clustered opposite active zone
● Fast NT (Glu, GABA, glycine) + neuropeptides; associative learning described
Shared ancestry
Possible independent origin (convergent evolution in ctenophores)
